summary: Matched and linked the Game & Watch Fire item unit. The PR promoted src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchfire.c from NonMatching to Matching, gave the item functions proper itGamewatchFire_* names, moved the item state table into the fire item C file, updated fighter AttackS4 torch callers and item logic table references, and cleaned stale generic it_27CF prototypes. decomp.dev reported +248 bytes matched code, +832 bytes linked code, +16 bytes matched data, and +32 bytes linked data, with itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim and the unit .data reaching 100%.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itgamewatchfire.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).

Renamed raw or logic-table-derived symbols for Game & Watch Fire to item-specific names: it_802C68F8 became itGamewatchFire_Spawn, it_2725_Logic73_Destroyed became itGamewatchFire_Destroyed, it_2725_Logic73_PickedUp became itGamewatchFire_PickedUp, it_2725_Logic73_EvtUnk became itGamewatchFire_EvtUnk, and itGamewatchfire_UnkMotion0_Anim became itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim.
Updated ftGw_AttackS4.c to include it/items/itgamewatchfire.h instead of it/items/it_27CF.h and call the newly named fire item APIs for spawn, damage cleanup, enter hitlag, and exit hitlag.
Updated the Game & Watch fire entry in it_803F3100 inside src/melee/it/it_2725.c to use itGamewatchFire_Destroyed, itGamewatchFire_PickedUp, and itGamewatchFire_EvtUnk.
Removed the Game & Watch Fire prototypes from the broader it_27CF.h and it_27CF.dox declarations after adding them to itgamewatchfire.h.
Added the Game & Watch Fire public prototypes to itgamewatchfire.h, including HSD_GObj, Vec3, Fighter_Part, and ItemStateTable declarations/imports needed by callers.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F78E8[] in itgamewatchfire.c with motion 0 wired to itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim, enabling the unit .data match reported by decomp.dev.
Reworked itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim to use GET_JOBJ, a static inline torchRemoveCheck helper, and itGamewatchFire_Destroyed for shared owner cleanup before returning true.

Updated GALE01 symbols so Game & Watch Fire functions use the new names, itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim is local, and several nearby sdata2 floats changed from global it_804DD2A8/AC/B0 names to local @148/@200/@201 labels.
smart_moves: The PR did not just force a match locally; it linked the unit by updating configure.py, symbol names, headers, fighter call sites, and the central item logic table together.
Moving function declarations from the catch-all it_27CF.h into itgamewatchfire.h made the ownership boundary clearer: Game & Watch AttackS4 now depends on the fire item header directly.
Factoring owner-removal logic into static inline torchRemoveCheck let itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim express the same control flow while also satisfying matching constraints.
Reusing itGamewatchFire_Destroyed inside itGamewatchFire_802C6A2C and the motion animation path avoided duplicating the owner flag cleanup and appears to have helped the decompiled code match.
Defining it_803F78E8 in the item source with the correct motion callback addressed a data match as well as the code match.
Changing generic symbols to item-scoped names made downstream references in ftGw_AttackS4.c and it_2725.c searchable and semantically tied to Game & Watch Fire.
decomp_lessons: When an item file reaches matching status, update the build configuration, symbol map, item logic table, and all cross-module callers in the same PR so the unit can link cleanly.
Prefer item-specific headers for item APIs used by fighter code; avoid keeping solved functions in broad placeholder headers such as it_27CF.h once a dedicated header exists.
Small control-flow helpers, including static inline functions, can be useful matching tools when they preserve behavior while giving the compiler a closer shape.
Centralize repeated side effects such as owner cleanup through the actual destroyed callback when that matches generated code and makes item lifecycle behavior clearer.
Data matching may require placing the ItemStateTable definition in the concrete item source and making sure the callback symbol locality matches the symbol map.
Update local/global symbol scopes alongside C visibility: this PR made itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim a local symbol while exposing only the cross-module APIs in the header.
naming_conventions: Use the item-prefixed form itGamewatchFire_* for Game & Watch Fire functions rather than raw address names or generic it_2725_Logic73_* names.
Use descriptive lifecycle suffixes when known: _Spawn, _Destroyed, _PickedUp, _Motion0_Anim, and _EvtUnk.

For unknown-but-item-specific helpers, this PR used the compromise form itGamewatchFire_802C6A2C, itGamewatchFire_802C6A78, and itGamewatchFire_802C6A98, preserving addresses while tying the functions to the owning item.
Keep callback names consistent with item capitalization: itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim, not the previous itGamewatchfire_UnkMotion0_Anim.
Keep local sdata2 constants as local @ labels when the symbol map evidence supports local scope rather than promoting them to global it_804D* names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted itgamewatchfire.c to Matching in configure.py only after the code/data changes made the unit match.
Introduced static inline bool torchRemoveCheck(Item_GObj*) to isolate the owner-null and ftGw_AttackS4_ItemCheckTorchRemove branch pattern used by the animation function.
Used GET_JOBJ(item_gobj) for the HSD_JObj pointer instead of reading item_gobj->hsd_obj into a temp, then passed that JObj to lb_8000BA0C when changing animation speed.
Replaced duplicated owner flag-setting checks with a call to itGamewatchFire_Destroyed, preserving the observable ftGw_AttackS4_ItemTorchSetFlag behavior when item->owner is non-null.
Matched the item state table data by defining it_803F78E8[] as a single-entry ItemStateTable with motion callback itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim and NULL physics/collision callbacks.
Adjusted symbols.txt to mark itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim as local and the exposed fire APIs as global.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 50.38% (+0.01%, +248 bytes), linked code 30.36% (+0.02%, +832 bytes), matched data 20.48% (+16 bytes), and linked data 28.73% (+32 bytes).
decomp.dev identified 2 new matches in main/melee/it/items/itgamewatchfire: itGamewatchFire_Motion0_Anim reached 100.00% and .data reached 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other Game & Watch item PRs that moved prototypes out of it_27CF.h into dedicated itgamewatch*.h headers.
Search for remaining it_2725_Logic7* item callbacks that can be renamed to item-specific lifecycle names.
Look for other item motion animation functions where a static inline removal-check helper could improve matching.
Check remaining Game & Watch item files still marked NonMatching in configure.py after itgamewatchfire.c was promoted.
Audit local/global symbol scope for item motion callbacks and nearby sdata2 constants in symbols.txt.
